findings: Independently verified identity: Great Blue Heron (Ardea herodias), family Ardeidae. The candidate’s blue-gray plumage, whitish head and neck, broad black eye/crown stripe with rear plumes, long yellowish straight bill, yellow eye, gray-to-yellowish legs, broad folded wings with darker flight feathers, and short tail agree with the supplied references and authoritative descriptions.
All stated measurements are supported: length 1.0–1.4 m, wingspan 1.7–2.0 m, and weight 2.1–2.5 kg. Cornell gives 97–137 cm, 167–201 cm, and 2,100–2,500 g; Audubon gives the stated rounded ranges.

Visible habitat and behavior claims agree with the verified accounts, including slow/statue-like shallow-water hunting, rapid bill strikes, S-curved/tucked neck in flight, broad wings, and legs extending beyond the short tail.
The illustration has one head, one beak, two wings (a folded pair), two legs, and one short tail. No duplicate or malformed anatomy is visible.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears. The scale is a generic metric ruler only.
No material species, anatomy, or text errors found.
